pattern: BFS on the 1-to-n² board squares with dice-roll branching; convert square labels to row/column using boustrophedon numbering.
hints: Model each square as a node; edges connect square s to the landing positions after rolling 1-6.
Convert a square label to (row, col) carefully: the bottom row goes left-to-right, the next right-to-left, alternating.
If a square has a snake or ladder, follow it immediately; BFS layer count gives the minimum dice rolls.

edge_inputs.description: 2x2 board, one ladder to last square
snake sending back on direct roll
unreachable destination due to snakes forming cycle
snake at square reachable in one move creates suboptimal path

stress.description: random board with random snakes and ladders, no cycles
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(20, size))
    total = n * n
    board = [[-1] * n for _ in range(n)]
    # Place a random subset of snakes/ladders ensuring no cycles
    candidates = list(range(2, total))  # squares 2..n^2-1 can have snakes/ladders
    rng.shuffle(candidates)
    used_src = set()
    used_dst = set()
    for sq in candidates[:max(0, total // 5)]:
        dst = rng.randint(1, total)
        if dst == sq or dst in used_dst or sq in used_src:
            continue
        # Convert sq to board coords
        sq0 = sq - 1
        row_from_bottom = sq0 // n
        col = sq0 % n
        if row_from_bottom % 2 == 1:
            col = n - 1 - col
        r = n - 1 - row_from_bottom
        board[r][col] = dst
        used_src.add(sq)
        used_dst.add(dst)
    return [board]
